title: Acianthera costabilis
intro: Acianthera costabilis, also known as Costabilis orchid, is a species of epiphytic orchid native to the tropical regions of South America. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Acianthera costabilis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including colds, coughs, and skin irritations.
taxonomy: Acianthera costabilis is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The genus Acianthera is a subgenus of the Pleurothallis genus. The classification of Acianthera costabilis is further categorized under the subfamily Epidendroideae. The subfamily is a part of the large and diverse Orchidaceae family.
uses: Acianthera costabilis is a species of orchid, and unfortunately, there isn't much information available on its medicinal uses.
